title: Creating Assertions
code:
-- Creating an Assertion (syntax varies by DBMS)
-- Example: No employee can have salary > manager's salary
CREATE ASSERTION salary_not_exceed_manager
CHECK (NOT EXISTS (
 SELECT e.emp_id 
 FROM employee e
 JOIN employee m ON e.manager_id = m.emp_id
 WHERE e.salary > m.salary
));
explanation: This assertion ensures that for any employee, their salary never exceeds their direct manager's salary. The check runs after any INSERT or UPDATE that could violate this rule.

title: BEFORE INSERT Trigger
explanation: This BEFORE INSERT trigger automatically generates an employee ID using a sequence and sets audit fields before the row is inserted. Uses :NEW to reference the new row values.

title: AFTER UPDATE Trigger with Conditional Logic
description: Log salary changes to audit table when salary increases by more than 20%
code:
CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER trg_log_salary_change
AFTER UPDATE OF salary ON employee
FOR EACH ROW
WHEN (NEW.salary > OLD.salary * 1.20)
BEGIN
 INSERT INTO salary_audit (emp_id, old_salary, new_salary, changed_by, change_date)
 VALUES (:OLD.emp_id, :OLD.salary, :NEW.salary, USER, SYSDATE);
END;
/
explanation: This AFTER UPDATE trigger logs significant salary changes (>20%) to an audit table. The WHEN clause makes it conditional - it only fires when the condition is met.

title: INSTEAD OF Trigger on View
description: Enable insertion through a view that joins multiple tables
code:
CREATE OR REPLACE TRIGGER trg_instead_of_insert_empdept
INSTEAD OF INSERT ON emp_dept_view
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
 -- Insert into department first
 INSERT INTO department (dept_id, dept_name)
 VALUES (:NEW.dept_id, :NEW.dept_name);
 
 -- Then insert into employee
 INSERT INTO employee (emp_id, emp_name, dept_id)
 VALUES (:NEW.emp_id, :NEW.emp_name, :NEW.dept_id);
END;
/
explanation: INSTEAD OF triggers allow DML operations on views that aren't normally updatable. This trigger intercepts INSERT on the view and performs the actual inserts into underlying tables.

title: Statement-Level Trigger for Business Rule
explanation: This statement-level trigger prevents any DELETE on the employee table during business hours. It fires once regardless of how many rows would be deleted.

title: Compound Trigger Example
description: Maintain running total across all timing points to avoid mutating table
explanation: Compound triggers (Oracle 11g+) can define actions for multiple timing points in one trigger. This solves the mutating table problem by allowing access to aggregate information after the statement completes.
